current_runtime_constraints: USDC-FUTURES and COIN-FUTURES productType routing is not represented by the shared market type model
Spot-Margin account, borrow, liability, and margin order semantics are not represented by the Spot runtime

official_sources.evidence: Bitget Futures API documents three product types for REST and WebSocket: USDT-FUTURES, USDC-FUTURES, and COIN-FUTURES.
The contract config endpoint requires productType and lists USDT-FUTURES, COIN-FUTURES, and USDC-FUTURES.
The futures account-list endpoint also scopes account readback by productType across USDT-FUTURES, COIN-FUTURES, and USDC-FUTURES.
Bitget documents a separate Spot-Margin API surface.

runtime_isolation: Do not promote USDC-FUTURES, COIN-FUTURES, or Spot-Margin from the existing Spot plus USDT-FUTURES runtime. Each product line needs explicit productType/profile guards, metadata and book fixtures, account/position or liability parsers, signed request specs, and post-write readback reconciliation.

usdc_futures: order book, ticker, trades, and funding for USDC-FUTURES
account balances and positions scoped to USDC-FUTURES
place, cancel, cancel-all, query, open-orders, and fills scoped to USDC-FUTURES
coin_futures: order book, ticker, trades, and funding or delivery data for COIN-FUTURES
place, cancel, cancel-all, query, open-orders, and fills scoped to COIN-FUTURES
margin: margin assets, liabilities, borrow/repay state, and risk state
margin place, cancel, query, open-orders, and fills

unsafe_to_promote_without: separate parser fixtures for USDC-FUTURES and COIN-FUTURES metadata/account/position/order responses
margin liability and borrow/repay parser fixtures
private signed request specs for each product line
post-write query/open/fills reconciliation per product line
